sourceMovie.title: TMNT
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto TMNT (2007) as a adventure theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The adventure and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Big Hero 6
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If TMNT clicked for you, Big Hero 6 (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Despicable Me 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If TMNT clicked for you, Despicable Me 3 (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after TMNT?
faq.answer: Try Big Hero 6, Despicable Me 3, The Lego Ninjago Movie—each shares DNA with TMNT while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is TMNT?
faq.answer: TMNT (2007) is best described as Adventure, Animation, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is TMNT worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
